verses.bsb: Then Satan rose up against Israel and incited David to take a census of Israel.

verses.esv: So David said to Joab and the commanders of the army, “Go, number Israel, from Beersheba to Dan, and bring me a report, that I may know their number.”
verses.kjv: And David said to Joab and to the rulers of the people, Go, number Israel from Beer-sheba even to Dan; and bring the number of them to me, that I may know it.
verses.bsb: So David said to Joab and the commanders of the troops, “Go and count the Israelites from Beersheba to Dan and bring me a report, so that I may know their number.”

verses.esv: But Joab said, “May the LORD add to his people a hundred times as many as they are! Are they not, my lord the king, all of them my lord's servants? Why then should my lord require this? Why should it be a cause of guilt for Israel?”
verses.kjv: And Joab answered, The Lord make his people an hundred times so many more as they be: but, my lord the king, are they not all my lord’s servants? why then doth my lord require this thing? why will he be a cause of trespass to Israel?
verses.bsb: But Joab replied, “May the LORD multiply His troops a hundred times over. My lord the king, are they not all servants of my lord? Why does my lord want to do this? Why should he bring guilt on Israel?”

verses.esv: But the king's word prevailed against Joab. So Joab departed and went throughout all Israel and came back to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the king’s word prevailed against Joab. Wherefore Joab departed, and went throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, the king’s word prevailed against Joab. So Joab departed and traveled throughout Israel, and then he returned to Jerusalem.

verses.esv: And Joab gave the sum of the numbering of the people to David. In all Israel there were 1,100,000 men who drew the sword, and in Judah 470,000 who drew the sword.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joab gave the sum of the number of the people unto David. And all they of Israel were a thousand thousand and an hundred thousand men that drew sword: and Judah was four hundred threescore and ten thousand men that drew sword.
verses.bsb: And Joab reported to David the total number of the troops. In all Israel there were 1,100,000 men who drew the sword, including 470,000 in Judah.

verses.esv: But he did not include Levi and Benjamin in the numbering, for the king's command was abhorrent to Joab.
verses.kjv: But Levi and Benjamin counted he not among them: for the king’s word was abominable to Joab.
verses.bsb: But Joab did not include Levi and Benjamin in the count, because the king’s command was detestable to him.

verses.esv: But God was displeased with this thing, and he struck Israel.
verses.kjv: And God was displeased with this thing; therefore he smote Israel.
verses.bsb: This command was also evil in the sight of God; so He struck Israel.

verses.esv: And David said to God, “I have sinned greatly in that I have done this thing. But now, please take away the iniquity of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.”
verses.kjv: And David said unto God, I have sinned greatly, because I have done this thing: but now, I beseech thee, do away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.
verses.bsb: Then David said to God, “I have sinned greatly because I have done this thing. Now I beg You to take away the iniquity of Your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Gad, David's seer, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Gad, David’s seer, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD instructed Gad, David’s seer,

verses.esv: “Go and say to David, ‘Thus says the LORD, Three things I offer you; choose one of them, that I may do it to you.’”
verses.kjv: Go and tell David, saying, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things: choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.
verses.bsb: “Go and tell David that this is what the LORD says: ‘I am offering you three options. Choose one of them, and I will carry it out against you.’”

verses.esv: So Gad came to David and said to him, “Thus says the LORD, ‘Choose what you will:
verses.kjv: So Gad came to David, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Choose thee
verses.bsb: So Gad went and said to David, “This is what the LORD says: ‘You must choose

verses.esv: either three years of famine, or three months of devastation by your foes while the sword of your enemies overtakes you, or else three days of the sword of the LORD, pestilence on the land, with the angel of the LORD destroying throughout all the territory of Israel.’ Now decide what answer I shall return to him who sent me.”
verses.kjv: Either three years’ famine; or three months to be destroyed before thy foes, while that the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee; or else three days the sword of the Lord, even the pestilence, in the land, and the angel of the Lord destroying throughout all the coasts of Israel. Now therefore advise thyself what word I shall bring again to him that sent me.
verses.bsb: between three years of famine, three months of being swept away before your enemies and overtaken by their swords, or three days of the sword of the LORD—days of plague upon the land, with the angel of the LORD ravaging every part of Israel.’ Now then, decide how I should reply to Him who sent me.”

verses.esv: Then David said to Gad, “I am in great distress. Let me fall into the hand of the LORD, for his mercy is very great, but do not let me fall into the hand of man.”
verses.kjv: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let me fall now into the hand of the Lord; for very great are his mercies: but let me not fall into the hand of man.
verses.bsb: David answered Gad, “I am deeply distressed. Please, let me fall into the hand of the LORD, for His mercies are very great; but do not let me fall into the hands of men.”

verses.esv: So the LORD sent a pestilence on Israel, and 70,000 men of Israel fell.
verses.kjv: ¶ So the Lord sent pestilence upon Israel: and there fell of Israel seventy thousand men.
verses.bsb: So the LORD sent a plague upon Israel, and seventy thousand men of Israel fell dead.

verses.esv: And God sent the angel to Jerusalem to destroy it, but as he was about to destroy it, the LORD saw, and he relented from the calamity. And he said to the angel who was working destruction, “It is enough; now stay your hand.” And the angel of the LORD was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.kjv: And God sent an angel unto Jerusalem to destroy it: and as he was destroying, the Lord beheld, and he repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed, It is enough, stay now thine hand. And the angel of the Lord stood by the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.bsb: Then God sent an angel to destroy Jerusalem, but as the angel was doing so, the LORD saw it and relented from the calamity, and He said to the angel who was destroying the people, “Enough! Withdraw your hand now!”  At that time the angel of the LORD was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

verses.esv: And David lifted his eyes and saw the angel of the LORD standing between earth and heaven, and in his hand a drawn sword stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell upon their faces.
verses.kjv: And David lifted up his eyes, and saw the angel of the Lord stand between the earth and the heaven, having a drawn sword in his hand stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders of Israel, who were clothed in sackcloth, fell upon their faces.
verses.bsb: When David lifted up his eyes and saw the angel of the LORD standing between heaven and earth, with a drawn sword in his hand stretched out over Jerusalem, David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell facedown.

verses.esv: And David said to God, “Was it not I who gave command to number the people? It is I who have sinned and done great evil. But these sheep, what have they done? Please let your hand, O LORD my God, be against me and against my father's house. But do not let the plague be on your people.”
verses.kjv: And David said unto God, Is it not I that commanded the people to be numbered? even I it is that have sinned and done evil indeed; but as for these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, O Lord my God, be on me, and on my father’s house; but not on thy people, that they should be plagued.
verses.bsb: And David said to God, “Was it not I who gave the order to count the people? I am the one who has sinned and acted wickedly. But these sheep, what have they done? O LORD my God, please let Your hand fall upon me and my father’s house, but do not let this plague remain upon Your people.”

verses.esv: Now the angel of the LORD had commanded Gad to say to David that David should go up and raise an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to say to David, that David should go up, and set up an altar unto the Lord in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.bsb: Then the angel of the LORD ordered Gad to tell David to go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

verses.esv: So David went up at Gad's word, which he had spoken in the name of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And David went up at the saying of Gad, which he spake in the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: So David went up at the word that Gad had spoken in the name of the LORD.

verses.esv: Now Ornan was threshing wheat. He turned and saw the angel, and his four sons who were with him hid themselves.
verses.kjv: And Ornan turned back, and saw the angel; and his four sons with him hid themselves. Now Ornan was threshing wheat.
verses.bsb: Now Ornan was threshing wheat when he turned and saw the angel; and his four sons who were with him hid themselves.

verses.esv: As David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David and went out from the threshing floor and paid homage to David with his face to the ground.
verses.kjv: And as David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David, and went out of the threshingfloor, and bowed himself to David with his face to the ground.
verses.bsb: David came to Ornan, and when Ornan looked out and saw David, he left the threshing floor and bowed facedown before David.

verses.esv: And David said to Ornan, “Give me the site of the threshing floor that I may build on it an altar to the LORD—give it to me at its full price—that the plague may be averted from the people.”
verses.kjv: Then David said to Ornan, Grant me the place of this threshingfloor, that I may build an altar therein unto the Lord: thou shalt grant it me for the full price: that the plague may be stayed from the people.
verses.bsb: Then David said to Ornan, “Grant me the site of this threshing floor, that I may build an altar to the LORD. Sell it to me for the full price, so that the plague upon the people may be halted.”

verses.esv: Then Ornan said to David, “Take it, and let my lord the king do what seems good to him. See, I give the oxen for burnt offerings and the threshing sledges for the wood and the wheat for a grain offering; I give it all.”
verses.kjv: And Ornan said unto David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do that which is good in his eyes: lo, I give thee the oxen also for burnt offerings, and the threshing instruments for wood, and the wheat for the meat offering; I give it all.
verses.bsb: Ornan said to David, “My lord the king may take whatever seems good. Look, I will give the oxen for the burnt offerings, the threshing sledges for the wood, and the wheat for the grain offering—I will give it all.”

verses.esv: But King David said to Ornan, “No, but I will buy them for the full price. I will not take for the LORD what is yours, nor offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”
verses.kjv: And king David said to Ornan, Nay; but I will verily buy it for the full price: for I will not take that which is thine for the Lord, nor offer burnt offerings without cost.
verses.bsb: “No,” replied King David, “I insist on paying the full price, for I will not take for the LORD what belongs to you, nor will I offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”

verses.esv: So David paid Ornan 600 shekels of gold by weight for the site.
verses.kjv: So David gave to Ornan for the place six hundred shekels of gold by weight.
verses.bsb: So David paid Ornan six hundred shekels of gold for the site.

verses.esv: And David built there an altar to the LORD and presented burnt offerings and peace offerings and called on the LORD, and the LORD answered him with fire from heaven upon the altar of burnt offering.
verses.kjv: And David built there an altar unto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings, and called upon the Lord; and he answered him from heaven by fire upon the altar of burnt offering.
verses.bsb: And there he built an altar to the LORD and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. He called upon the LORD, who answered him with fire from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.

verses.esv: Then the LORD commanded the angel, and he put his sword back into its sheath.
verses.kjv: And the Lord commanded the angel; and he put up his sword again into the sheath thereof.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD spoke to the angel, who put his sword back into its sheath.

verses.esv: At that time, when David saw that the LORD had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he sacrificed there.
verses.kjv: ¶ At that time when David saw that the Lord had answered him in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.
verses.bsb: At that time, when David saw that the LORD had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he offered sacrifices there.

verses.esv: For the tabernacle of the LORD, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt offering were at that time in the high place at Gibeon,
verses.kjv: For the tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses made in the wilderness, and the altar of the burnt offering, were at that season in the high place at Gibeon.
verses.bsb: For the tabernacle of the LORD that Moses had made in the wilderness and the altar of burnt offering were presently at the high place in Gibeon,

verses.esv: but David could not go before it to inquire of God, for he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the LORD. Then David said, “Here shall be the house of the LORD God and here the altar of burnt offering for Israel.”
verses.kjv: But David could not go before it to inquire of God: for he was afraid because of the sword of the angel of the Lord.
verses.bsb: but David could not go before it to inquire of God, because he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the LORD.

verses.esv: David commanded to gather together the resident aliens who were in the land of Israel, and he set stonecutters to prepare dressed stones for building the house of God.
verses.kjv: And David commanded to gather together the strangers that were in the land of Israel; and he set masons to hew wrought stones to build the house of God.
verses.bsb: So David gave orders to gather the foreigners in the land of Israel, from whom he appointed stonecutters to prepare finished stones for building the house of God.

verses.esv: David also provided great quantities of iron for nails for the doors of the gates and for clamps, as well as bronze in quantities beyond weighing,
verses.kjv: And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails for the doors of the gates, and for the joinings; and brass in abundance without weight;
verses.bsb: David provided a large quantity of iron to make the nails for the doors of the gateways and for the fittings, together with more bronze than could be weighed

verses.esv: and cedar timbers without number, for the Sidonians and Tyrians brought great quantities of cedar to David.
verses.kjv: Also cedar trees in abundance: for the Zidonians and they of Tyre brought much cedar wood to David.
verses.bsb: and more cedar logs than could be counted; for the Sidonians and Tyrians had brought a large quantity of cedar logs to David.

verses.esv: For David said, “Solomon my son is young and inexperienced, and the house that is to be built for the LORD must be exceedingly magnificent, of fame and glory throughout all lands. I will therefore make preparation for it.” So David provided materials in great quantity before his death.
verses.kjv: And David said, Solomon my son is young and tender, and the house that is to be builded for the Lord must be exceeding magnifical, of fame and of glory throughout all countries: I will therefore now make preparation for it. So David prepared abundantly before his death.
verses.bsb: And David said, “My son Solomon is young and inexperienced, and the house to be built for the LORD must be exceedingly magnificent—famous and glorious throughout all lands. Therefore I must make preparations for it.” So David made lavish preparations before his death.

verses.esv: Then he called for Solomon his son and charged him to build a house for the LORD, the God of Israel.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then he called for Solomon his son, and charged him to build an house for the Lord God of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then David called for his son Solomon and instructed him to build a house for the LORD, the God of Israel.

verses.esv: David said to Solomon, “My son, I had it in my heart to build a house to the name of the LORD my God.
verses.kjv: And David said to Solomon, My son, as for me, it was in my mind to build an house unto the name of the Lord my God:
verses.bsb: “My son,” said David to Solomon, “it was in my heart to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God,

verses.esv: But the word of the LORD came to me, saying, ‘You have shed much blood and have waged great wars. You shall not build a house to my name, because you have shed so much blood before me on the earth.
verses.kjv: But the word of the Lord came to me, saying, Thou hast shed blood abundantly, and hast made great wars: thou shalt not build an house unto my name, because thou hast shed much blood upon the earth in my sight.
verses.bsb: but this word of the LORD came to me: ‘You have shed much blood and waged great wars. You are not to build a house for My Name because you have shed so much blood on the ground before Me.

verses.esv: Behold, a son shall be born to you who shall be a man of rest. I will give him rest from all his surrounding enemies. For his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quiet to Israel in his days.
verses.kjv: Behold, a son shall be born to thee, who shall be a man of rest; and I will give him rest from all his enemies round about: for his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quietness unto Israel in his days.
verses.bsb: But a son will be born to you who will be a man of rest. I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side; for his name will be Solomon, and I will grant to Israel peace and quiet during his reign.

verses.esv: He shall build a house for my name. He shall be my son, and I will be his father, and I will establish his royal throne in Israel forever.’
verses.kjv: He shall build an house for my name; and he shall be my son, and I will be his father; and I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel for ever.
verses.bsb: He is the one who will build a house for My Name. He will be My son, and I will be his Father. And I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel forever.’

verses.esv: “Now, my son, the LORD be with you, so that you may succeed in building the house of the LORD your God, as he has spoken concerning you.
verses.kjv: Now, my son, the Lord be with thee; and prosper thou, and build the house of the Lord thy God, as he hath said of thee.
verses.bsb: Now, my son, may the LORD be with you, and may you succeed in building the house of the LORD your God, as He said you would.

verses.esv: Only, may the LORD grant you discretion and understanding, that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: Only the Lord give thee wisdom and understanding, and give thee charge concerning Israel, that thou mayest keep the law of the Lord thy God.
verses.bsb: Above all, may the LORD give you insight and understanding when He puts you in command over Israel, so that you may keep the Law of the LORD your God.

verses.esv: Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the rules that the LORD commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and courageous. Fear not; do not be dismayed.
verses.kjv: Then shalt thou prosper, if thou takest heed to fulfil the statutes and judgments which the Lord charged Moses with concerning Israel: be strong, and of good courage; dread not, nor be dismayed.
verses.bsb: Then you will succeed, if you carefully follow the statutes and ordinances that the LORD commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or discouraged.

verses.esv: With great pains I have provided for the house of the LORD 100,000 talents of gold, a million talents of silver, and bronze and iron beyond weighing, for there is so much of it; timber and stone, too, I have provided. To these you must add.
verses.kjv: Now, behold, in my trouble I have prepared for the house of the Lord an hundred thousand talents of gold, and a thousand thousand talents of silver; and of brass and iron without weight; for it is in abundance: timber also and stone have I prepared; and thou mayest add thereto.
verses.bsb: Now behold, I have taken great pains to provide for the house of the LORD—100,000 talents of gold, 1,000,000 talents of silver, and bronze and iron too great to be weighed. I have also provided timber and stone, and you may add to them.

verses.esv: You have an abundance of workmen: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and all kinds of craftsmen without number, skilled in working
verses.kjv: Moreover there are workmen with thee in abundance, hewers and workers of stone and timber, and all manner of cunning men for every manner of work.
verses.bsb: You also have many workers: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and men skilled in every kind of work—

verses.esv: gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Arise and work! The LORD be with you!”
verses.kjv: Of the gold, the silver, and the brass, and the iron, there is no number. Arise therefore, and be doing, and the Lord be with thee.
verses.bsb: in gold and silver, bronze and iron—craftsmen beyond number. Now begin the work, and may the LORD be with you.”

verses.esv: David also commanded all the leaders of Israel to help Solomon his son, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ David also commanded all the princes of Israel to help Solomon his son, saying,
verses.bsb: Then David ordered all the leaders of Israel to help his son Solomon:

verses.esv: “Is not the LORD your God with you? And has he not given you peace on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hand, and the land is subdued before the LORD and his people.
verses.kjv: Is not the Lord your God with you? and hath he not given you rest on every side? for he hath given the inhabitants of the land into mine hand; and the land is subdued before the Lord, and before his people.
verses.bsb: “Is not the LORD your God with you, and has He not granted you rest on every side? For He has given the inhabitants of the land into my hand, and the land has been subdued before the LORD and His people.

verses.esv: Now set your mind and heart to seek the LORD your God. Arise and build the sanctuary of the LORD God, so that the ark of the covenant of the LORD and the holy vessels of God may be brought into a house built for the name of the LORD.”
verses.kjv: Now set your heart and your soul to seek the Lord your God; arise therefore, and build ye the sanctuary of the Lord God, to bring the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and the holy vessels of God, into the house that is to be built to the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Now set your heart and soul to seek the LORD your God. Get started building the sanctuary of the LORD God, so that you may bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD and the holy articles of God into the temple that will be built for the Name of the LORD.”

verses.esv: When David was old and full of days, he made Solomon his son king over Israel.
verses.kjv: SO when David was old and full of days, he made Solomon his son king over Israel.
verses.bsb: When David was old and full of years, he installed his son Solomon as king over Israel.

verses.esv: David assembled all the leaders of Israel and the priests and the Levites.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he gathered together all the princes of Israel, with the priests and the Levites.
verses.bsb: Then he gathered all the leaders of Israel, as well as the priests and Levites.

verses.esv: The Levites, thirty years old and upward, were numbered, and the total was 38,000 men.
verses.kjv: Now the Levites were numbered from the age of thirty years and upward: and their number by their polls, man by man, was thirty and eight thousand.
verses.bsb: The Levites thirty years of age or older were counted, and the total number of men was 38,000.

verses.esv: “Twenty-four thousand of these,” David said, “shall have charge of the work in the house of the LORD, 6,000 shall be officers and judges,
verses.kjv: Of which, twenty and four thousand were to set forward the work of the house of the Lord; and six thousand were officers and judges:
verses.bsb: “Of these,” said David, “24,000 are to oversee the work of the house of the LORD, 6,000 are to be officers and judges,

verses.esv: 4,000 gatekeepers, and 4,000 shall offer praises to the LORD with the instruments that I have made for praise.”
verses.kjv: Moreover four thousand were porters; and four thousand praised the Lord with the instruments which I made, said David, to praise therewith.
verses.bsb: 4,000 are to be gatekeepers, and 4,000 are to praise the LORD with the instruments I have made for giving praise.”

verses.esv: And David organized them in divisions corresponding to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
verses.kjv: And David divided them into courses among the sons of Levi, namely, Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
verses.bsb: Then David divided the Levites into divisions according to the sons of Levi:  Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

verses.esv: The sons of Gershon were Ladan and Shimei.
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the Gershonites were, Laadan, and Shimei.
verses.bsb: The Gershonites: Ladan and Shimei.

verses.esv: The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the chief, and Zetham, and Joel, three.
verses.kjv: The sons of Laadan; the chief was Jehiel, and Zetham, and Joel, three.
verses.bsb: The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the first, Zetham, and Joel—three in all.

verses.esv: The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the heads of the fathers' houses of Ladan.
verses.kjv: The sons of Shimei; Shelomith, and Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the chief of the fathers of Laadan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran—three in all. These were the heads of the families of Ladan.

verses.esv: And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, and Jeush and Beriah. These four were the sons of Shimei.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Shimei were, Jahath, Zina, and Jeush, and Beriah. These four were the sons of Shimei.
verses.bsb: And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah. These were the sons of Shimei—four in all.

verses.esv: Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second; but Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, therefore they became counted as a single father's house.
verses.kjv: And Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second: but Jeush and Beriah had not many sons; therefore they were in one reckoning, according to their father’s house.
verses.bsb: Jahath was the first and Zizah was the second; but Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, so they were counted as one family and received a single assignment.

verses.esv: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Kohath; Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.
verses.bsb: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel—four in all.

verses.esv: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to dedicate the most holy things, that he and his sons forever should make offerings before the LORD and minister to him and pronounce blessings in his name forever.
verses.kjv: The sons of Amram; Aaron and Moses: and Aaron was separated, that he should sanctify the most holy things, he and his sons for ever, to burn incense before the Lord, to minister unto him, and to bless in his name for ever.
verses.bsb: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron and his descendants were set apart forever to consecrate the most holy things, to burn incense before the LORD, to minister before Him, and to pronounce blessings in His name forever.

verses.esv: But the sons of Moses the man of God were named among the tribe of Levi.
verses.kjv: Now concerning Moses the man of God, his sons were named of the tribe of Levi.
verses.bsb: As for Moses the man of God, his sons were named among the tribe of Levi.

verses.esv: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
verses.kjv: The sons of Moses were, Gershom, and Eliezer.
verses.bsb: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.

verses.esv: The sons of Gershom: Shebuel the chief.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Gershom, Shebuel was the chief.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Gershom: Shebuel was the first.

verses.esv: The sons of Eliezer: Rehabiah the chief. Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very many.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Eliezer were, Rehabiah the chief. And Eliezer had none other sons; but the sons of Rehabiah were very many.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Eliezer: Rehabiah was the first. Eliezer did not have any other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very numerous.

verses.esv: The sons of Izhar: Shelomith the chief.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Izhar; Shelomith the chief.
verses.bsb: The sons of Izhar: Shelomith was the first.

verses.esv: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Hebron; Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
verses.bsb: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah was the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

verses.esv: The sons of Uzziel: Micah the chief and Isshiah the second.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Uzziel; Michah the first, and Jesiah the second.
verses.bsb: The sons of Uzziel: Micah was the first and Isshiah the second.

verses.esv: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Merari; Mahli, and Mushi. The sons of Mahli; Eleazar, and Kish.
verses.bsb: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi.

verses.esv: Eleazar died having no sons, but only daughters; their kinsmen, the sons of Kish, married them.
verses.kjv: And Eleazar died, and had no sons, but daughters: and their brethren the sons of Kish took them.
verses.bsb: The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish. Eleazar died without having any sons; he had only daughters. Their cousins, the sons of Kish, married them.

verses.esv: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth, three.
verses.kjv: The sons of Mushi; Mahli, and Eder, and Jeremoth, three.
verses.bsb: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth—three in all.

verses.esv: These were the sons of Levi by their fathers' houses, the heads of fathers' houses as they were listed according to the number of the names of the individuals from twenty years old and upward who were to do the work for the service of the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ These were the sons of Levi after the house of their fathers; even the chief of the fathers, as they were counted by number of names by their polls, that did the work for the service of the house of the Lord, from the age of twenty years and upward.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Levi by their families—the heads of families, registered individually by name—those twenty years of age or older who worked in the service of the house of the LORD.

verses.esv: For David said, “The LORD, the God of Israel, has given rest to his people, and he dwells in Jerusalem forever.
verses.kjv: For David said, The Lord God of Israel hath given rest unto his people, that they may dwell in Jerusalem for ever:
verses.bsb: For David had said, “The LORD, the God of Israel, has given rest to His people and has come to dwell in Jerusalem forever.

verses.esv: And so the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the things for its service.”
verses.kjv: And also unto the Levites; they shall no more carry the tabernacle, nor any vessels of it for the service thereof.
verses.bsb: So now the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the articles for its service.”

verses.esv: For by the last words of David the sons of Levi were numbered from twenty years old and upward.
verses.kjv: For by the last words of David the Levites were numbered from twenty years old and above:
verses.bsb: For according to the final instructions of David, the Levites twenty years of age or older were counted,

verses.esv: For their duty was to assist the sons of Aaron for the service of the house of the LORD, having the care of the courts and the chambers, the cleansing of all that is holy, and any work for the service of the house of God.
verses.kjv: Because their office was to wait on the sons of Aaron for the service of the house of the Lord, in the courts, and in the chambers, and in the purifying of all holy things, and the work of the service of the house of God;
verses.bsb: but their duty was to assist the descendants of Aaron with the service of the house of the LORD, being responsible for the courts and chambers, the purification of all the holy things, and the work of the service of the house of God,

verses.esv: Their duty was also to assist with the showbread, the flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baked offering, the offering mixed with oil, and all measures of quantity or size.
verses.kjv: Both for the shewbread, and for the fine flour for meat offering, and for the unleavened cakes, and for that which is baked in the pan, and for that which is fried, and for all manner of measure and size;
verses.bsb: as well as for the rows of the showbread, the fine flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baking, the mixing, and all measurements of quantity and size.

verses.esv: And they were to stand every morning, thanking and praising the LORD, and likewise at evening,
verses.kjv: And to stand every morning to thank and praise the Lord, and likewise at even;
verses.bsb: They were also to stand every morning to give thanks and praise to the LORD, and likewise in the evening.

verses.esv: and whenever burnt offerings were offered to the LORD on Sabbaths, new moons, and feast days, according to the number required of them, regularly before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And to offer all burnt sacrifices unto the Lord in the sabbaths, in the new moons, and on the set feasts, by number, according to the order commanded unto them, continually before the Lord:
verses.bsb: Whenever burnt offerings were presented to the LORD on the Sabbaths, New Moons, and appointed feasts, they were to serve regularly before the LORD in the numbers prescribed for them.

verses.esv: Thus they were to keep charge of the tent of meeting and the sanctuary, and to attend the sons of Aaron, their brothers, for the service of the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And that they should keep the charge of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the charge of the holy place, and the charge of the sons of Aaron their brethren, in the service of the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: So the Levites were to carry out the responsibilities for the Tent of Meeting and the Holy Place, and, under their brothers the descendants of Aaron, the service of the house of the LORD.

verses.esv: The divisions of the sons of Aaron were these. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.kjv: NOW these are the divisions of the sons of Aaron. The sons of Aaron; Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.bsb: These were the divisions of the descendants of Aaron. The sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

verses.esv: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father and had no children, so Eleazar and Ithamar became the priests.
verses.kjv: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no children: therefore Eleazar and Ithamar executed the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father did, and they had no sons; so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests.

verses.esv: With the help of Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, David organized them according to the appointed duties in their service.
verses.kjv: And David distributed them, both Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, according to their offices in their service.
verses.bsb: With the help of Eleazar’s descendant Zadok and Ithamar’s descendant Ahimelech, David divided them according to the offices of their service.

verses.esv: Since more chief men were found among the sons of Eleazar than among the sons of Ithamar, they organized them under sixteen heads of fathers' houses of the sons of Eleazar, and eight of the sons of Ithamar.
verses.kjv: And there were more chief men found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar; and thus were they divided. Among the sons of Eleazar there were sixteen chief men of the house of their fathers, and eight among the sons of Ithamar according to the house of their fathers.
verses.bsb: Since more leaders were found among Eleazar’s descendants than those of Ithamar, they were divided accordingly. There were sixteen heads of families from the descendants of Eleazar and eight from the descendants of Ithamar.

verses.esv: They divided them by lot, all alike, for there were sacred officers and officers of God among both the sons of Eleazar and the sons of Ithamar.
verses.kjv: Thus were they divided by lot, one sort with another; for the governors of the sanctuary, and governors of the house of God, were of the sons of Eleazar, and of the sons of Ithamar.
verses.bsb: Thus they were divided by lot, for there were officers of the sanctuary and officers of God among both Eleazar’s and Ithamar’s descendants.

verses.esv: And the scribe Shemaiah, the son of Nethanel, a Levite, recorded them in the presence of the king and the princes and Zadok the priest and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar and the heads of the fathers' houses of the priests and of the Levites, one father's house being chosen for Eleazar and one chosen for Ithamar.
verses.kjv: And Shemaiah the son of Nethaneel the scribe, one of the Levites, wrote them before the king, and the princes, and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, and before the chief of the fathers of the priests and Levites: one principal household being taken for Eleazar, and one taken for Ithamar.
verses.bsb: The scribe, Shemaiah son of Nethanel, a Levite, recorded their names in the presence of the king and of the officers: Zadok the priest, Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the heads of families of the priests and the Levites—one family being taken from Eleazar, and then one from Ithamar.

verses.esv: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
verses.kjv: Now the first lot came forth to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
verses.bsb: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib,   the second to Jedaiah,

verses.esv: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
verses.kjv: The third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
verses.bsb: the third to Harim,   the fourth to Seorim,

verses.esv: the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
verses.kjv: The fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
verses.bsb: the fifth to Malchijah,   the sixth to Mijamin,

verses.esv: the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
verses.kjv: The seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
verses.bsb: the seventh to Hakkoz,   the eighth to Abijah,

verses.esv: the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
verses.kjv: The ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
verses.bsb: the ninth to Jeshua,   the tenth to Shecaniah,

verses.esv: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
verses.kjv: The eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
verses.bsb: the eleventh to Eliashib,   the twelfth to Jakim,

verses.esv: the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
verses.kjv: The thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
verses.bsb: the thirteenth to Huppah,   the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,

verses.esv: the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
verses.kjv: The fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
verses.bsb: the fifteenth to Bilgah,   the sixteenth to Immer,

verses.esv: the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,
verses.kjv: The seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Aphses,
verses.bsb: the seventeenth to Hezir,   the eighteenth to Happizzez,

verses.esv: the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,
verses.kjv: The nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezekel,
verses.bsb: the nineteenth to Pethahiah,   the twentieth to Jehezkel,

verses.esv: the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
verses.kjv: The one and twentieth to Jachin, the two and twentieth to Gamul,
verses.bsb: the twenty-first to Jachin,   the twenty-second to Gamul,

verses.esv: the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
verses.kjv: The three and twentieth to Delaiah, the four and twentieth to Maaziah.
verses.bsb: the twenty-third to Delaiah,   and the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.

verses.esv: These had as their appointed duty in their service to come into the house of the LORD according to the procedure established for them by Aaron their father, as the LORD God of Israel had commanded him.
verses.kjv: These were the orderings of them in their service to come into the house of the Lord, according to their manner, under Aaron their father, as the Lord God of Israel had commanded him.
verses.bsb: This was their appointed order for service when they entered the house of the LORD, according to the regulations prescribed for them by their forefather Aaron, as the LORD, the God of Israel, had commanded him.

verses.esv: And of the rest of the sons of Levi: of the sons of Amram, Shubael; of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the rest of the sons of Levi were these: Of the sons of Amram; Shubael: of the sons of Shubael; Jehdeiah.
verses.bsb: Now these were the remaining descendants of Levi:  From the sons of Amram: Shubael;   from the sons of Shubael: Jehdeiah.

verses.esv: Of Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, Isshiah the chief.
verses.kjv: Concerning Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, the first was Isshiah.
verses.bsb: As for Rehabiah, from his sons: The first was Isshiah.

verses.esv: Of the Izharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath.
verses.kjv: Of the Izharites; Shelomoth: of the sons of Shelomoth; Jahath.
verses.bsb: From the Izharites: Shelomoth;   from the sons of Shelomoth: Jahath.

verses.esv: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Hebron; Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
verses.bsb: From the sons of Hebron: Jeriah was the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

verses.esv: The sons of Uzziel, Micah; of the sons of Micah, Shamir.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Uzziel; Michah: of the sons of Michah; Shamir.
verses.bsb: From the sons of Uzziel: Micah;   from the sons of Micah: Shamir.

verses.esv: The brother of Micah, Isshiah; of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
verses.kjv: The brother of Michah was Isshiah: of the sons of Isshiah; Zechariah.
verses.bsb: The brother of Micah: Isshiah;   from the sons of Isshiah: Zechariah.

verses.esv: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Jaaziah: Beno.
verses.kjv: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi: the sons of Jaaziah; Beno.
verses.bsb: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi.   The son of Jaaziah: Beno.

verses.esv: The sons of Merari: of Jaaziah, Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Merari by Jaaziah; Beno, and Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Merari from Jaaziah: Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.

verses.esv: Of Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
verses.kjv: Of Mahli came Eleazar, who had no sons.
verses.bsb: From Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.

verses.esv: Of Kish, the sons of Kish: Jerahmeel.
verses.kjv: Concerning Kish: the son of Kish was Jerahmeel.
verses.bsb: From Kish: Jerahmeel the son of Kish.

verses.esv: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their fathers' houses.
verses.kjv: The sons also of Mushi; Mahli, and Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites after the house of their fathers.
verses.bsb: And the sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth.   These were the sons of the Levites, according to their families.

verses.esv: These also, the head of each father's house and his younger brother alike, cast lots, just as their brothers the sons of Aaron, in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of fathers' houses of the priests and of the Levites.
verses.kjv: These likewise cast lots over against their brethren the sons of Aaron in the presence of David the king, and Zadok, and Ahimelech, and the chief of the fathers of the priests and Levites, even the principal fathers over against their younger brethren.
verses.bsb: As their brothers the descendants of Aaron did, they also cast lots in the presence of King David and of Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of the families of the priests and Levites—the family heads and their younger brothers alike.

verses.esv: David and the chiefs of the service also set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who prophesied with lyres, with harps, and with cymbals. The list of those who did the work and of their duties was:
verses.kjv: MOREOVER David and the captains of the host separated to the service of the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals: and the number of the workmen according to their service was:
verses.bsb: Additionally, David and the commanders of the army set apart some of the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun to prophesy with the accompaniment of lyres, harps, and cymbals. The following is the list of the men who performed this service:

verses.esv: Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asharelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Asaph; Zaccur, and Joseph, and Nethaniah, and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph under the hands of Asaph, which prophesied according to the order of the king.
verses.bsb: From the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah. These sons of Asaph were under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.

verses.esv: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the lyre in thanksgiving and praise to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Of Jeduthun: the sons of Jeduthun; Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the hands of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with a harp, to give thanks and to praise the Lord.
verses.bsb: From the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six in all—under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the harp, giving thanks and praise to the LORD.

verses.esv: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
verses.kjv: Of Heman: the sons of Heman; Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth:
verses.bsb: From the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.

verses.esv: All these were the sons of Heman the king's seer, according to the promise of God to exalt him, for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
verses.kjv: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer in the words of God, to lift up the horn. And God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
verses.bsb: All these sons of Heman the king’s seer were given him through the promises of God to exalt him, for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

verses.esv: They were all under the direction of their father in the music in the house of the LORD with cymbals, harps, and lyres for the service of the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the order of the king.
verses.kjv: All these were under the hands of their father for song in the house of the Lord, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps, for the service of the house of God, according to the king’s order to Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.
verses.bsb: All these were under the direction of their fathers for the music of the house of the LORD with cymbals, harps, and lyres, for the service of the house of God.  Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the direction of the king.

verses.esv: The number of them along with their brothers, who were trained in singing to the LORD, all who were skillful, was 288.
verses.kjv: So the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of the Lord, even all that were cunning, was two hundred fourscore and eight.
verses.bsb: Together with their relatives, who were all trained and skillful in the songs of the LORD, they numbered 288.

verses.esv: And they cast lots for their duties, small and great, teacher and pupil alike.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they cast lots, ward against ward, as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.
verses.bsb: They cast lots for their duties, young and old alike, teacher as well as pupil.

verses.esv: The first lot fell for Asaph to Joseph; the second to Gedaliah, to him and his brothers and his sons, twelve;
verses.kjv: Now the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph: the second to Gedaliah, who with his brethren and sons were twelve:
verses.bsb: The first lot, which was for Asaph, fell to Joseph, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;   the second to Gedaliah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the third to Zaccur, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The third to Zaccur, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the third to Zaccur, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the fourth to Izri, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The fourth to Izri, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the fourth to Izri, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the fifth to Nethaniah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The fifth to Nethaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the fifth to Nethaniah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the sixth to Bukkiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The sixth to Bukkiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the sixth to Bukkiah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the seventh to Jesharelah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The seventh to Jesharelah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the seventh to Jesarelah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the eighth to Jeshaiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The eighth to Jeshaiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the eighth to Jeshaiah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the ninth to Mattaniah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The ninth to Mattaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the ninth to Mattaniah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the tenth to Shimei, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The tenth to Shimei, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the tenth to Shimei, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the eleventh to Azarel, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The eleventh to Azareel, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the eleventh to Azarel, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: the twelfth to Hashabiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The twelfth to Hashabiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the twelfth to Hashabiah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the thirteenth, Shubael, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The thirteenth to Shubael, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the thirteenth to Shubael, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the fourteenth, Mattithiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The fourteenth to Mattithiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the fourteenth to Mattithiah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the fifteenth, to Jeremoth, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The fifteenth to Jeremoth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the fifteenth to Jeremoth, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the sixteenth, to Hananiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The sixteenth to Hananiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the sixteenth to Hananiah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the seventeenth, to Joshbekashah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The seventeenth to Joshbekashah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the eighteenth, to Hanani, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The eighteenth to Hanani, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the eighteenth to Hanani, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the nineteenth, to Mallothi, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The nineteenth to Mallothi, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the nineteenth to Mallothi, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the twentieth, to Eliathah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The twentieth to Eliathah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the twentieth to Eliathah, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the twenty-first, to Hothir, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The one and twentieth to Hothir, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the twenty-first to Hothir, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the twenty-second, to Giddalti, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The two and twentieth to Giddalti, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the twenty-second to Giddalti, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the twenty-third, to Mahazioth, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
verses.kjv: The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
verses.bsb: the twenty-third to Mahazioth, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;

verses.esv: to the twenty-fourth, to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his brothers, twelve.
verses.kjv: The four and twentieth to Romamti-ezer, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve.
verses.bsb: and the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all.

verses.esv: As for the divisions of the gatekeepers: of the Korahites, Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
verses.kjv: CONCERNING the divisions of the porters: Of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
verses.bsb: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers:  From the Korahites: Meshelemiah son of Kore, one of the sons of Asaph.

verses.esv: And Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
verses.kjv: And the sons of Meshelemiah were, Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
verses.bsb: Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,

verses.esv: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
verses.kjv: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.
verses.bsb: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, and Eliehoenai the seventh.

verses.esv: And Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
verses.kjv: Moreover the sons of Obed-edom were, Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth,
verses.bsb: And Obed-edom also had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,

verses.esv: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai the eighth, for God blessed him.
verses.kjv: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth: for God blessed him.
verses.bsb: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, and Peullethai the eighth. For God had blessed Obed-edom.

verses.esv: Also to his son Shemaiah were sons born who were rulers in their fathers' houses, for they were men of great ability.
verses.kjv: Also unto Shemaiah his son were sons born, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.
verses.bsb: Also to his son Shemaiah were born sons who ruled over their families because they were strong, capable men.

verses.esv: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, Obed and Elzabad, whose brothers were able men, Elihu and Semachiah.
verses.kjv: The sons of Shemaiah; Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men, Elihu, and Semachiah.
verses.bsb: Shemaiah’s sons were Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad; his brothers were Elihu and Semachiah, also capable men.

verses.esv: All these were of the sons of Obed-edom with their sons and brothers, able men qualified for the service; sixty-two of Obed-edom.
verses.kjv: All these of the sons of Obed-edom: they and their sons and their brethren, able men for strength for the service, were threescore and two of Obed-edom.
verses.bsb: All these were descendants of Obed-edom; they and their sons and brothers were capable men with strength to do the work—62 in all from Obed-edom.

verses.esv: And Meshelemiah had sons and brothers, able men, eighteen.
verses.kjv: And Meshelemiah had sons and brethren, strong men, eighteen.
verses.bsb: Meshelemiah also had sons and brothers who were capable men—18 in all.

verses.esv: And Hosah, of the sons of Merari, had sons: Shimri the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),
verses.kjv: Also Hosah, of the children of Merari, had sons; Simri the chief, (for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father made him the chief;)
verses.bsb: Hosah the Merarite also had sons: Shimri the first (although he was not the firstborn, his father had appointed him as the first),

verses.esv: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brothers of Hosah were thirteen.
verses.kjv: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brethren of Hosah were thirteen.
verses.bsb: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, and Zechariah the fourth. The sons and brothers of Hosah numbered 13 in all.

verses.esv: These divisions of the gatekeepers, corresponding to their chief men, had duties, just as their brothers did, ministering in the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: Among these were the divisions of the porters, even among the chief men, having wards one against another, to minister in the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: These divisions of the gatekeepers, through their chief men, had duties for ministering in the house of the LORD, just as their brothers did.

verses.esv: And they cast lots by fathers' houses, small and great alike, for their gates.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they cast lots, as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers, for every gate.
verses.bsb: They cast lots for each gate, according to their families, young and old alike.

verses.esv: The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah. They cast lots also for his son Zechariah, a shrewd counselor, and his lot came out for the north.
verses.kjv: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counseller, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward.
verses.bsb: The lot for the East Gate fell to Shelemiah.   Then lots were cast for his son Zechariah, a wise counselor, and the lot for the North Gate fell to him.

verses.esv: Obed-edom's came out for the south, and to his sons was allotted the gatehouse.
verses.kjv: To Obed-edom southward; and to his sons the house of Asuppim.
verses.bsb: The lot for the South Gate fell to Obed-edom, and the lot for the storehouses to his sons.

verses.esv: For Shuppim and Hosah it came out for the west, at the gate of Shallecheth on the road that goes up. Watch corresponded to watch.
verses.kjv: To Shuppim and Hosah the lot came forth westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the going up, ward against ward.
verses.bsb: The lots for the West Gate and the Shallecheth Gate on the ascending highway fell to Shuppim and Hosah.   There were guards stationed at every watch.

verses.esv: On the east there were six each day, on the north four each day, on the south four each day, as well as two and two at the gatehouse.
verses.kjv: Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and toward Asuppim two and two.
verses.bsb: Each day there were six Levites on the east, four on the north, four on the south, and two pairs at the storehouse.

verses.esv: And for the colonnade on the west there were four at the road and two at the colonnade.
verses.kjv: At Parbar westward, four at the causeway, and two at Parbar.
verses.bsb: As for the court on the west, there were four at the highway and two at the court.

verses.esv: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the Korahites and the sons of Merari.
verses.kjv: These are the divisions of the porters among the sons of Kore, and among the sons of Merari.
verses.bsb: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers who were descendants of Korah and Merari.

verses.esv: And of the Levites, Ahijah had charge of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of the dedicated gifts.
verses.kjv: ¶ And of the Levites, Ahijah was over the treasures of the house of God, and over the treasures of the dedicated things.
verses.bsb: Now their fellow Levites were in charge of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of the dedicated things.

verses.esv: The sons of Ladan, the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan, the heads of the fathers' houses belonging to Ladan the Gershonite: Jehieli.
verses.kjv: As concerning the sons of Laadan; the sons of the Gershonite Laadan, chief fathers, even of Laadan the Gershonite, were Jehieli.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Ladan, who were Gershonites through Ladan and heads of the families of Ladan the Gershonite, were Jehieli,

verses.esv: The sons of Jehieli, Zetham, and Joel his brother, were in charge of the treasuries of the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: The sons of Jehieli; Zetham, and Joel his brother, which were over the treasures of the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: the sons of Jehieli, Zetham, and his brother Joel. They were in charge of the treasuries of the house of the LORD.

verses.esv: Of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites—
verses.kjv: Of the Amramites, and the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
verses.bsb: From the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:

verses.esv: and Shebuel the son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief officer in charge of the treasuries.
verses.kjv: And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was ruler of the treasures.
verses.bsb: Shebuel, a descendant of Gershom son of Moses, was the officer in charge of the treasuries.

verses.esv: His brothers: from Eliezer were his son Rehabiah, and his son Jeshaiah, and his son Joram, and his son Zichri, and his son Shelomoth.
verses.kjv: And his brethren by Eliezer; Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.
verses.bsb: His relatives through Eliezer included Rehabiah his son, Jeshaiah his son, Joram his son, Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.

verses.esv: This Shelomoth and his brothers were in charge of all the treasuries of the dedicated gifts that David the king and the heads of the fathers' houses and the officers of the thousands and the hundreds and the commanders of the army had dedicated.
verses.kjv: Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king, and the chief fathers, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the host, had dedicated.
verses.bsb: This Shelomith and his brothers were in charge of all the treasuries for the things dedicated by King David, by the heads of families who were the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and by the army commanders.

verses.esv: From spoil won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: Out of the spoils won in battles did they dedicate to maintain the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: They had dedicated some of the plunder from their battles to the repair of the house of the LORD.

verses.esv: Also all that Samuel the seer and Saul the son of Kish and Abner the son of Ner and Joab the son of Zeruiah had dedicated—all dedicated gifts were in the care of Shelomoth and his brothers.
verses.kjv: And all that Samuel the seer, and Saul the son of Kish, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Zeruiah, had dedicated; and whosoever had dedicated any thing, it was under the hand of Shelomith, and of his brethren.
verses.bsb: Everything that had been dedicated by Samuel the seer, Saul son of Kish, Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah, along with everything else that was dedicated, was under the care of Shelomith and his brothers.

verses.esv: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were appointed to external duties for Israel, as officers and judges.
verses.kjv: ¶ Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were for the outward business over Israel, for officers and judges.
verses.bsb: From the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons had the outside duties as officers and judges over Israel.

verses.esv: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brothers, 1,700 men of ability, had the oversight of Israel westward of the Jordan for all the work of the LORD and for the service of the king.
verses.kjv: And of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Jordan westward in all the business of the Lord, and in the service of the king.
verses.bsb: From the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his relatives, 1,700 capable men, had charge of the affairs of Israel west of the Jordan for all the work of the LORD and for the service of the king.

verses.esv: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah was chief of the Hebronites of whatever genealogy or fathers' houses. (In the fortieth year of David's reign search was made and men of great ability among them were found at Jazer in Gilead.)
verses.kjv: Among the Hebronites was Jerijah the chief, even among the Hebronites, according to the generations of his fathers. In the fortieth year of the reign of David they were sought for, and there were found among them mighty men of valour at Jazer of Gilead.
verses.bsb: As for the Hebronites, Jerijah was the chief of the Hebronites, according to the genealogies of his ancestors. In the fortieth year of David’s reign the records were searched, and strong, capable men were found among the Hebronites at Jazer in Gilead.

verses.esv: King David appointed him and his brothers, 2,700 men of ability, heads of fathers' houses, to have the oversight of the Reubenites, the Gadites and the half-tribe of the Manassites for everything pertaining to God and for the affairs of the king.
verses.kjv: And his brethren, men of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred chief fathers, whom king David made rulers over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh, for every matter pertaining to God, and affairs of the king.
verses.bsb: Among Jerijah’s relatives there were 2,700 capable men who were heads of families. King David appointed them over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh for every matter pertaining to God and to the affairs of the king.

verses.esv: This is the number of the people of Israel, the heads of fathers' houses, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who served the king in all matters concerning the divisions that came and went, month after month throughout the year, each division numbering 24,000:
verses.kjv: NOW the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in any matter of the courses, which came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year, of every course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: This is the list of the Israelites—the heads of families, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and their officers who served the king in every matter concerning the divisions on rotating military duty each month throughout the year. There were 24,000 men in each division:

verses.esv: Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division in the first month; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, which was assigned the first month. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: He was a descendant of Perez and was chief of all the commanders. He served for the first month.
verses.kjv: Of the children of Perez was the chief of all the captains of the host for the first month.
verses.bsb: He was a descendant of Perez and chief of all the army commanders for the first month.

verses.esv: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division of the second month; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: And over the course of the second month was Dodai an Ahohite, and of his course was Mikloth also the ruler: in his course likewise were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division for the second month, and Mikloth was the leader. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: The third commander, for the third month, was Benaiah, the son of Jehoiada the chief priest; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The third army commander, as chief for the third month, was Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: This is the Benaiah who was a mighty man of the thirty and in command of the thirty; Ammizabad his son was in charge of his division.
verses.kjv: This is that Benaiah, who was mighty among the thirty, and above the thirty: and in his course was Ammizabad his son.
verses.bsb: This Benaiah was mighty among the Thirty and was over the Thirty, and his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.

verses.esv: Asahel the brother of Joab was fourth, for the fourth month, and his son Zebadiah after him; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The fourth captain for the fourth month was Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son after him: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Joab’s brother Asahel, and his son Zebadiah was commander after him. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: The fifth commander, for the fifth month, was Shamhuth the Izrahite; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The fifth captain for the fifth month was Shamhuth the Izrahite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The fifth, for the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth the Izrahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira, the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The sixth captain for the sixth month was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the sons of Ephraim; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The seventh captain for the seventh month was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, an Ephraimite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The eighth captain for the eighth month was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, a Zerahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer of Anathoth, a Benjaminite; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The ninth captain for the ninth month was Abiezer the Anetothite, of the Benjamites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, a Benjamite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai of Netophah, of the Zerahites; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The tenth captain for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, a Zerahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah of Pirathon, of the sons of Ephraim; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The eleventh captain for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, an Ephraimite. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel; in his division were 24,000.
verses.kjv: The twelfth captain for the twelfth month was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
verses.bsb: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, from the family of Othniel. There were 24,000 men in his division.

verses.esv: Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites, Eliezer the son of Zichri was chief officer; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maacah;
verses.kjv: ¶ Furthermore over the tribes of Israel: the ruler of the Reubenites was Eliezer the son of Zichri: of the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maachah:
verses.bsb: These officers were in charge of the tribes of Israel:  Over the Reubenites was Eliezer son of Zichri;   over the Simeonites was Shephatiah son of Maacah;

verses.esv: for Levi, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;
verses.kjv: Of the Levites, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel: of the Aaronites, Zadok:
verses.bsb: over Levi was Hashabiah son of Kemuel;   over Aaron was Zadok;

verses.esv: for Judah, Elihu, one of David's brothers; for Issachar, Omri the son of Michael;
verses.kjv: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David: of Issachar, Omri the son of Michael:
verses.bsb: over Judah was Elihu, one of David’s brothers;   over Issachar was Omri son of Michael;

verses.esv: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jeremoth the son of Azriel;
verses.kjv: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah: of Naphtali, Jerimoth the son of Azriel:
verses.bsb: over Zebulun was Ishmaiah son of Obadiah;   over Naphtali was Jerimoth son of Azriel;

verses.esv: for the Ephraimites, Hoshea the son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah;
verses.kjv: Of the children of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Azaziah: of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah:
verses.bsb: over the Ephraimites was Hoshea son of Azaziah;   over one of the half-tribes of Manasseh was Joel son of Pedaiah;

verses.esv: for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner;
verses.kjv: Of the half tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the son of Zechariah: of Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner:
verses.bsb: over the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead was Iddo son of Zechariah;   over Benjamin was Jaasiel son of Abner;

verses.esv: for Dan, Azarel the son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
verses.kjv: Of Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.
verses.bsb: and over Dan was Azarel son of Jeroham.   These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.

verses.esv: David did not count those below twenty years of age, for the LORD had promised to make Israel as many as the stars of heaven.
verses.kjv: ¶ But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the Lord had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens.
verses.bsb: David did not count the men aged twenty or under, because the LORD had said that He would make Israel as numerous as the stars of the sky.

verses.esv: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to count, but did not finish. Yet wrath came upon Israel for this, and the number was not entered in the chronicles of King David.
verses.kjv: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel; neither was the number put in the account of the chronicles of king David.
verses.bsb: Joab son of Zeruiah began to count the men but did not finish. For because of this census wrath came upon Israel, and the number was not entered in the Book of the Chronicles of King David.

verses.esv: Over the king's treasuries was Azmaveth the son of Adiel; and over the treasuries in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers, was Jonathan the son of Uzziah;
verses.kjv: ¶ And over the king’s treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel: and over the storehouses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Jehonathan the son of Uzziah:
verses.bsb: Azmaveth son of Adiel was in charge of the royal storehouses.   Jonathan son of Uzziah was in charge of the storehouses in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the fortresses.
